𝗛𝗙𝗦 | To easily share files and folders from a personal computer over a local network or the internet via a web browser, acting as a personal cloud storage and sharing solution.  🔥 Super Hot!

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To easily share files and folders from a personal computer over a local network or the internet via a web browser, acting as a personal cloud storage and sharing solution.
📅 Created: Dec 2021 • Updated: Mar 2026

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Web-based file sharing
✅ Virtual file system
✅ Real-time activity monitoring
✅ Bandwidth throttling
✅ Direct file transfers
✅ Plugin support
✅ HTTPS support
✅ Resumable downloads & uploads
✅ Mobile-friendly interface
✅ Admin web interface


✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 3.6K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 336 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 20 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

HFS (HTTP File Server) is a versatile web-based file server that allows users to share files and folders directly from their computer over the web. It offers features like a virtual file system for flexible sharing, real-time monitoring, bandwidth throttling, and plugin support for extensibility.

✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦✦

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/rejetto/hfs

𝗟𝗔𝗦𝗧𝗦𝗜𝗚𝗡𝗔𝗟 | To provide a secure, self-hosted mechanism for users to ensure their important encrypted messages are delivered to loved ones or trusted contacts in the event of their incapacitation or death.  🔥 Super Hot!

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 💎 Ruby 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: To provide a secure, self-hosted mechanism for users to ensure their important encrypted messages are delivered to loved ones or trusted contacts in the event of their incapacitation or death.
📅 Created: Jan 2026 • Updated: Mar 2026

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Self-hosted dead man's switch
✅ End-to-end encrypted (E2EE) messaging
✅ Zero-knowledge architecture
✅ Email-first check-in system
✅ Configurable timing for check-ins and reminders
✅ Trusted contact notification system
✅ Recipient-specific message decryption delay
✅ Modern cryptography (Argon2id, XChaCha20-Poly1305, X25519)
✅ Auditable open-source code


▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐ 444 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 8 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 1 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

LastSignal is a self-hosted, end-to-end encrypted dead man's switch that automatically delivers messages to trusted contacts if the user becomes unresponsive. It prioritizes security with a zero-knowledge architecture and modern cryptography, allowing users to configure check-in intervals and delivery delays.

▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫▫

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/giovantenne/lastsignal

𝗣𝗗𝗙-𝗘𝗫𝗧𝗥𝗔𝗖𝗧-𝗞𝗜𝗧 | Extracting high-quality content and structured data from diverse and complex PDF documents, enabling the development of applications like document translation, Q&A, and assistants.  🔥 Super Hot!

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 🐍 Python 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Extracting high-quality content and structured data from diverse and complex PDF documents, enabling the development of applications like document translation, Q&A, and assistants.
📅 Created: Jun 2024 • Updated: Mar 2026

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Integration of Leading Document Parsing Models
✅ High-Quality Parsing Across Diverse Documents
✅ Modular Design
✅ Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmarks


◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐ 9.4K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 711 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 96 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

PDF-Extract-Kit is a Python toolkit for high-quality content extraction from complex PDF documents. It integrates state-of-the-art models for layout detection, formula recognition, OCR, and table recognition, offering a modular design for flexible application building and comprehensive evaluation benchmarks.

◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈◈

𝗢𝗣𝗘𝗡𝗨𝗜 | Rapid prototyping and generation of UI components through natural language descriptions, making UI development faster and more flexible.  🔥 Super Hot!

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

𝗧𝗘𝗖𝗛 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗖𝗞: 📘 TypeScript 
🎯 𝗨𝗦𝗘 𝗖𝗔𝗦𝗘: Rapid prototyping and generation of UI components through natural language descriptions, making UI development faster and more flexible.
📅 Created: Mar 2024 • Updated: Mar 2026

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

✨ 𝗞𝗘𝗬 𝗙𝗘𝗔𝗧𝗨𝗥𝗘𝗦 ✨

✅ Describe UI using imagination (natural language)
✅ Live rendering of generated UI
✅ Convert HTML to React, Svelte, Web Components, etc.
✅ Supports multiple LLM providers (OpenAI, Groq, Gemini, Anthropic, Cohere, Mistral)
✅ Local execution via Docker or from source
✅ Customizable LLM configuration via LiteLLM


┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

📊 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧 𝗦𝗧𝗔𝗧𝗦

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 22.1K 𝘀𝘁𝗮𝗿𝘀
🍴 2K 𝗳𝗼𝗿𝗸𝘀
🔍 65 𝗼𝗽𝗲𝗻 𝗶𝘀𝘀𝘂𝗲𝘀

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

📝 𝗔𝗕𝗢𝗨𝗧 𝗧𝗛𝗜𝗦 𝗣𝗥𝗢𝗝𝗘𝗖𝗧

OpenUI is an open-source tool that allows users to describe user interfaces using natural language and see them rendered live. It leverages AI to generate UI components and can convert them into various frontend frameworks like React, Svelte, and Web Components.

┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅┅

🔗 𝗟𝗜𝗡𝗞𝗦
• 𝗚𝗶𝘁𝗛𝘂𝗯: https://github.com/wandb/openui
